home.seoTitle: Hermetia — Your soul map. A portrait that understands you.
home.seoDescription: Hermetia turns your birth data into a soul map drawn from 31 interpretive systems and walks beside you day by day. Discover your profile for free.

hero.lead: Hermetia reads your birth data and weaves a single, clear soul map from up to 31 interpretive systems — and accompanies you day by day. Not a data sheet. A journey to yourself.

hero.quote: Your sensitivity is a compass — not a weakness.

items.a: Your soul map is an integrated personality portrait that Hermetia calculates from your birth data and up to 31 interpretive systems, woven into one understandable, beautiful view.

items.q: Do I need my exact birth time?
items.a: No. Hermetia works without an exact time — you simply pick a time of day. We tell you transparently which part of your profile is reliable even without the time.

items.q: Is this a diagnosis or a prediction?
items.a: No. Hermetia is inspiration for self-reflection — not medical, psychological or therapeutic advice, and not a prediction of fate.
Your data is stored encrypted in the EU. Spiritual-philosophical profiles are special data (GDPR Art. 9) — we ask for your explicit consent and you can export or delete everything at any time.

methodik.seoTitle: How your profile is made — How Hermetia calculates and interprets
methodik.seoDescription: Transparently explained: how Hermetia calculates a profile from your birth data (Skyfield), connects it with the convergence engine and interprets it warmly — and what the AI does and does not do.
methodik.title: We calculate precisely. We interpret warmly.
methodik.lead: Trust grows from transparency. So we show you exactly where every statement comes from — and where an AI writes along.

steps.title: Calculate — the solid ground
steps.text: From birth date, time and place a deterministic engine (the open-source ephemeris Skyfield) computes the exact positions of sun, moon and planets and the finite foundation of every system. Same data → same result. Nothing is guessed.

steps.title: Connect — the convergence engine
steps.text: All systems feed a shared map of themes. A pure calculation engine (no AI) measures where systems confirm one another — that becomes your strength — and where they diverge — that becomes a good question. Many voices become one.

steps.title: Accompany — the warm voice
steps.text: Only now does an AI phrase your interpretation — in your language, inviting rather than prescriptive. It reads only the calculated facts and the map of themes, never your raw birth date. Every AI text is visibly labelled.

layers.label: Layer A
layers.title: Foundations per system
layers.text: A complete, lasting foundational profile per system — the basis for everything.

layers.label: Layer B
layers.title: Core profile & levels
layers.text: From the synergy of all systems emerge your ten navigable levels.

layers.label: Layer C
layers.title: Daily derivation
layers.text: From your core profile + daily data (transits, moon phase) comes your gentle daily impulse.

aiDo: translate calculated facts into warm, readable language
phrase your level texts and daily impulses
write in your language, inviting and non-deterministic
methodik.aiDontTitle: What the AI does not do
aiDont: no statements about fate or fear
the AI model never sees your raw birth date

sections.heading: EU dispute resolution
sections.body: The European Commission provides a platform for online dispute resolution (ODR): https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/. Our email address is above. We are neither willing nor obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

sections.heading: Liability for content and links
sections.body: As a service provider we are responsible for our own content on these pages under general law. The operators of linked external sites are solely responsible for their content; no legal violations were apparent at the time of linking. We remove such links immediately upon becoming aware of any infringement.

sections.heading: Copyright
sections.body: The content and works created by us on these pages are subject to German copyright law. Third-party contributions are marked as such.

sections.heading: 1 · Controller
sections.body: The controller for data processing is [Provider / company name], [address], email: datenschutz@hermetia.de. [Where appointed:] Data protection officer: [name, contact].

sections.heading: 2 · Short overview
sections.body:
Hermetia creates a personal reflection profile from your inputs and calculated system markers. This includes the soul map, daily impulses, optional journaling, relationship and resonance modules, profile refinement, export functions, a possible full profile book and AI-assisted companion texts. These contents can be highly personal, even though they are not intended as diagnosis, therapy or binding advice.

We therefore do not treat this data like ordinary marketing data. Data minimisation, purpose limitation, clear consent, EU-oriented hosting, encryption, role-based access, export and deletion are part of the privacy concept. This policy is a careful launch draft and must be legally reviewed and aligned with the actual providers used before public release.

sections.heading: 3 · Which data we process
sections.body:
Depending on use, we process the following categories of data:

• Account data: email address, password hash, language setting, login status, plan and profile status.
• Profile and birth data: birth name or display name, date of birth, optional birth time, place of birth, time zone and location resolution, plus derived calculations.
• Derived profile content: soul map, system markers, convergence values, theme clusters, daily impulses, profile book sections and relationship interpretations.
• Voluntary context data: journaling entries, reflection answers, profile refinements, preferences, notes and released Companion contexts.
• Relationship data: inputs about other people only where a reliable consent and use model exists.
• Usage and security data: technical logs, session information, error data, abuse protection, consent status and basic interaction data.
• Payment data: processed by payment providers; we do not store full card details ourselves.

sections.heading: 4 · Special categories of personal data
sections.body:
Hermetia works with spiritual, worldview-related and personality-adjacent interpretations. Depending on the concrete design, such derived content may touch special categories of personal data under Art. 9 GDPR. We therefore treat this content with particular care and, where required, base processing on explicit, separate consent.

This consent must be clear, voluntary, informed and withdrawable. It must not be hidden in general terms of use. Users need to understand that Hermetia derives personal interpretations from inputs and may use them for profile functions, premium content, daily impulses, export or Companion features. Withdrawal applies for the future; processing that was lawful before withdrawal remains unaffected.

sections.heading: 5 · Purposes and legal bases
sections.body:
We process data only for defined purposes:

• Account, login, language, plan and technical provision: performance of a contract or pre-contractual measures.
• Profile calculation, soul map, system evaluation and convergence: contract performance and, where special categories are affected, explicit consent.
• Daily impulses, journaling, Companion, full profile book and profile refinement: contract performance, consent for sensitive content and legitimate interest in a safe product function.
• Payment processing, invoices and tax retention: contract performance and legal obligations.
• Security, error analysis, abuse prevention and access protection: legitimate interest.
• Newsletter, email impulses and optional product communication: consent unless a legal exception applies.
• Analytics and marketing measurement: only with consent unless purely technically required.

sections.heading: 6 · AI processing
sections.body:
Hermetia may use AI to phrase calculated profile markers, theme clusters and released contexts in understandable language. Calculation of system values should remain separate from AI wording. AI should not produce independent diagnosis, therapy, legal or financial advice, or binding decisions.

Only the information required for the specific purpose should be sent to AI services. Where possible, raw data is reduced, pseudonymised or replaced with calculated markers. Especially sensitive content such as journal entries or relationship data must not be used as unlimited or automatic AI context. Further details are provided on the AI transparency page.

sections.heading: 7 · Third-country transfers and providers
sections.body:
We use technical providers only where necessary for operation, security, payment, communication, analytics or AI functions. These may include hosting providers, payment services, email services, error analysis, consent management and AI providers. Where providers act as processors, agreements under Art. 28 GDPR are concluded.

If personal data is processed outside the EU or EEA, this must be based on a valid legal mechanism. This may include an adequacy decision, EU Standard Contractual Clauses, additional safeguards and a transfer risk assessment. Before launch, the concrete provider list must be documented with provider name, purpose, location, legal basis and contract status.

sections.heading: 8 · Relationships and data of other people
sections.body:
Relationship and resonance features may involve data about other people. Such features require particular care because nobody should be secretly evaluated in a spiritual or personality-adjacent way. Hermetia should therefore design relationship profiles so that consent, transparency and purpose limitation remain practical.

If data about a second person is entered, it must be clear on what basis this happens, who is responsible and which information is stored. Features for partnership, friendship, family or professional resonance must not encourage hidden assessment of other people. The legal and ethical design of these modules should receive a separate review before launch.

sections.heading: 9 · Retention, deletion and export
sections.body:
We store personal data only for as long as it is required for the stated purposes, consent exists, a contract is active or statutory retention periods apply. Profile content, journal texts and derived interpretations should be deletable unless legal duties prevent this.

Users should be able to export their essential profile data. This strengthens data portability, trust and control. When an account is deleted, the profile, derived content and voluntary context data are deleted or anonymised where technically and legally possible. Backups may remain for a limited period for security reasons, but are not actively reused.

sections.heading: 10 · Your rights
sections.body:
Subject to the GDPR, you have rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, data portability, objection and withdrawal of consent. You may also lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority.

Hermetia should make these rights practically accessible, not merely mention them formally. This includes profile export, deletion, consent management, transparent AI notices and understandable settings for voluntary data. If you have questions about your data, you can contact the address listed above.

sections.heading: 11 · Cookies, consent and analytics
sections.body:
The marketing website should use as few technically necessary cookies or comparable technologies as possible. Technically necessary elements may serve language settings, security, consent status or stable page delivery. Optional analytics, retargeting or conversion measurement may only be used where valid consent exists.

Until the final domain move and launch, the website intentionally remains on noindex/nofollow. This protects unfinished content from premature search indexing. After launch, measurement should enable reach, SEO, GEO and AEO without selling sensitive profile content for advertising or unnecessarily passing it to third parties.

sections.heading: 12 · Security
sections.body:
Hermetia should use technical and organisational measures appropriate to the risk of personal profile content. These include encrypted transmission, secure password storage, role-based access, logging of security-relevant events, backups, update processes, access restrictions, separated environments and deletion concepts.

It is especially important that production profile content does not flow uncontrolled into test systems, prompt experiments, screenshots, support tools or analytics environments. For sensitive AI functions, prompt logging, access to journal texts and debug output should be particularly limited.

sections.heading: 13 · No disclosure for advertising purposes
sections.body:
We do not sell personal profile content, journal texts, relationship data or derived interpretations to advertising partners. Data must not be used to address people manipulatively based on sensitive spiritual or personality-adjacent statements.

Marketing may rely on aggregated, technical or consent-based signals, but not on exploiting private reflection content. This separation is central to Hermetia: the website should grow without compromising the confidentiality of personal profiles.

sections.heading: 14 · Minors and sensitive situations
sections.body:
Hermetia is not directed at children. If functions for younger users, family contexts or particularly vulnerable situations are planned, separate age, consent and protection concepts are required. Spiritual or personality-adjacent content can have a stronger effect in crisis, grief, separation or health-related stress.

Hermetia should therefore provide clear notices, boundaries and escalation logic. The product must not replace emergency support and should point users in acute crisis situations to suitable professional services.

sections.heading: 15 · Changes and final review
sections.body:
We update this privacy policy when features, providers, legal bases, data flows or the legal situation change. The current version is always available on this page.

Before public launch, placeholders must be removed, providers named specifically, processor agreements checked, third-country transfers assessed, consent flows tested and the full policy approved by legal counsel. This page is an extensive privacy draft, but not final legal advice.

sections.heading: 1 · Scope and provider
sections.body: These terms apply to the use of Hermetia, offered by [Provider / company name], [address] (“we”). The version valid at the time the contract is concluded applies.

sections.heading: 2 · Subject of the service
sections.body: Hermetia is a digital service for self-reflection: it calculates a personal profile from birth data and provides interpretations, daily impulses and further features. There is a free tier (“Discover”) and paid subscriptions (“Companion”, “Depth”). The content is inspiration for self-reflection and not medical, psychological, therapeutic, legal or financial advice.

sections.heading: 3 · Conclusion of contract
sections.body: A free usage contract is concluded upon registration. You enter into a paid subscription by selecting the relevant plan and completing the order via our payment provider.

sections.heading: 4 · Prices and payment
sections.body: The prices displayed at the time of ordering apply (incl. statutory VAT where shown). Payment is made via the payment provider Stripe using the payment methods offered there. Annual subscriptions are billed in advance.

sections.heading: 5 · Term and cancellation
sections.body: Subscriptions run for the chosen period (monthly or yearly) and renew for the same period unless cancelled. You can cancel at any time with one click via the cancellation button in your profile (§ 312k BGB). Cancellation takes effect at the end of the period already paid for; fees already paid are not refunded pro rata unless mandatory law provides otherwise.

sections.heading: 6 · Right of withdrawal
sections.body: Consumers have a statutory right of withdrawal. Details and the model withdrawal form are in our withdrawal policy.

sections.heading: 7 · AI content and disclaimer
sections.body: Parts of the content are generated with the help of AI and are labelled (EU AI Act Art. 50). They do not replace professional advice. If you notice signs of physical or mental strain, please consult qualified professionals.

sections.heading: 8 · Minimum age
sections.body: Use requires a minimum age of 16; paid subscriptions require a minimum age of 18 or the consent of a legal guardian.

sections.heading: 9 · Liability
sections.body: We are liable without limitation for intent and gross negligence and for injury to life, body and health. Otherwise we are liable only for breach of essential contractual obligations, limited to the foreseeable damage typical for this type of contract.

sections.heading: 10 · Changes to the terms
sections.body: We may change these terms for good cause with reasonable advance notice. If you do not object within the deadline, the change is deemed accepted; we will separately inform you of the right to object and its consequences.

sections.heading: 11 · Final provisions
sections.body: German law applies, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods; mandatory consumer protection provisions of your country of residence remain unaffected. If a provision is invalid, the rest of the contract remains effective.

sections.heading: Right of withdrawal
sections.body: You have the right to withdraw from this contract within fourteen days without giving any reason. The withdrawal period is fourteen days from the day the contract is concluded.

sections.heading: Exercising the right
sections.body: To exercise your right of withdrawal, you must inform us ([Provider], [address], email: widerruf@hermetia.de) of your decision by means of a clear statement (e.g. by email). You may use the model withdrawal form below, but it is not mandatory. To meet the deadline, it is sufficient to send your notification before the period expires.

sections.heading: Consequences of withdrawal
sections.body: If you withdraw from this contract, we will refund all payments received from you without undue delay and at the latest within fourteen days. We use the same means of payment as in the original transaction unless otherwise agreed; you will not incur any fees for this.

sections.heading: Early expiry for digital content
sections.body: Your right of withdrawal expires early if we have begun providing the digital content after you expressly consented to us beginning before the end of the withdrawal period and you confirmed your awareness that you thereby lose your right of withdrawal.

sections.heading: Model withdrawal form
sections.body:
(If you wish to withdraw from the contract, complete this form and return it.)

— To [Provider], [address], widerruf@hermetia.de:
— I/we hereby withdraw from the contract concluded by me/us for the following service: Hermetia subscription
— Ordered on / received on: __________
— Name of consumer(s): __________
— Address: __________
— Date and signature (only if notified on paper): __________

sections.heading: Cancelling ongoing contracts
sections.body: Independently of the right of withdrawal, you can end an ongoing subscription at any time via the cancellation button in your profile (§ 312k BGB).

sections.heading: Why Hermetia discloses AI transparency
sections.body:
Hermetia combines deterministic calculations from several interpretation systems with linguistic refinement supported by AI. This combination can feel highly personal because it touches themes such as self-image, relationships, life rhythm, resonance and inner patterns. For that reason, we explain clearly where AI is involved, which data it processes, which limits apply and where human responsibility remains.

This transparency page is part of the legal and editorial foundation of the website. It supports the requirements of the EU AI Act, especially the labelling of AI-generated or AI-assisted content, and it gives users a clear basis before starting a profile, buying premium content or using Companion, journaling and the full profile book.

sections.heading: Where AI is used in Hermetia
sections.body:
AI is used in Hermetia primarily for wording, condensation and dialogue. This can include personal interpretation texts, layer descriptions, daily impulses, explanatory sections in the full profile book, reflection questions, Companion answers and editorial variants for different use cases. AI helps to express calculated patterns in a calm, understandable and usable way.

AI does not decide which system is right, which statement is true or which future will happen. It writes from prepared contexts, rules and calculated profile markers. Whenever a page, profile area or dialogue is phrased with AI assistance, this should be recognisable. Hermetia uses AI as a linguistic tool within defined boundaries, not as an invisible authority.

sections.heading: Calculation, convergence and wording are separate
sections.body:
A core safeguard in Hermetia is the separation of three layers: calculation, convergence and wording. The calculation layer classifies inputs such as birth data, system parameters and optional context according to defined rules from the systems used. The convergence engine then checks which motifs repeat across multiple systems, which statements should be treated as strong, medium or cautious, and where contradictions should remain visible.

Only after that does AI enter as a wording layer. Its task is not to invent a new truth, but to phrase already structured results in accessible language. This separation reduces the risk that a language model produces seemingly certain statements that are not actually supported by the underlying systems or method.

sections.heading: Which data AI processes
sections.body:
The AI model receives only the information required for the specific feature. Depending on the feature, this may include calculated profile markers, theme clusters, confidence levels, released journal notes, selected Companion contexts or already condensed profile sections. Hermetia follows the principle of data minimisation: as much context as needed, as little raw data as possible.

AI should not unnecessarily process full legal names, direct contact details, payment information or unrestricted raw profiles. Especially sensitive content such as journal entries or relationship information is only used in AI-assisted functions where it is required and covered by the usage context. The goal is a helpful personal dialogue without turning every detail into automatic training or analysis context.

sections.heading: What AI explicitly does not do
sections.body:
AI content in Hermetia is not a diagnosis, not therapy, not medical advice, not psychological treatment, not legal advice, not financial advice and not a binding life decision. Hermetia does not tell users whether to start or end a relationship, whether they have an illness, which career they must choose or whether a financial step is right.

The content is intended for self-reflection. It can give language to patterns, open questions and support inner observation. It does not replace qualified professionals or personal responsibility. If content feels emotionally intense, unexpectedly burdensome or touches concrete risks, external professional support should take priority.

sections.heading: Companion, journaling and personal dialogues
sections.body:
The Hermetia Companion is planned as a calm AI guide that answers within the user’s own profile context. It should not be a general chat AI, but should draw on released Hermetia contexts: profile book, daily impulses, journaling reflections, relationship interpretations and personal development fields. This can make its answers more connected than those of a generic language model.

At the same time, this feature needs clear boundaries. The Companion should make transparent which source an answer is derived from, whether a statement is strong or cautious and when it should not continue. Journal entries must not be used automatically without limits. Users must be able to understand which context is used and how they can control, delete or withhold sensitive material.

sections.heading: Premium content and fair conversion
sections.body:
AI can make premium content more readable, personal and continuous. A full profile book, deeper daily impulses or Companion dialogues create value because they translate calculated patterns into longer, more differentiated language. Still, AI must not be used to create artificial pressure, fear or urgency in order to push users into a paid model.

Hermetia should spark curiosity and invite sign-up, but the decision to choose a paid model must remain understandable. Good conversion comes from clarity, usefulness, trust and real depth, not from manipulative scarcity or apparently absolute claims. Premium arguments should therefore explain what expands: scope, context, continuity, dialogue depth, export, comparison and long-term work with the profile.

sections.heading: Copyright, sources and original language
sections.body:
Hermetia’s AI texts must be written in original language. They must not reproduce protected works, copied system descriptions, long third-party passages or trademark-sensitive phrasing. When interpretation systems are mentioned, they are described, compared and editorially contextualised in Hermetia’s own words.

The same principle applies to images, graphics and models: only proprietary material, licensed assets, self-created graphics or clearly approved project assets should be used in production. Before public launch, terms, product names, system names, image rights and trademark references should receive an additional legal review.

sections.heading: Human oversight and editorial control
sections.body:
AI outputs are not treated as infallible. Prompts, system rules, safety boundaries, tone and content logic need editorial maintenance. Especially sensitive topics such as health, relationships, crisis, grief, sexuality, minors, financial decisions or legal questions require additional boundaries and clear notices.

Before launch, central AI text patterns, onboarding flows, premium arguments and Companion answer logic should be reviewed by qualified experts. This is not only about legal risk, but also about tone, clarity, ethical effect and whether users can clearly recognise what is inspiration and what is not.

sections.heading: Current launch status
sections.body:
During the preparation phase, the website intentionally remains on noindex/nofollow because the URL will still move and all content needs final review before public launch. This setting prevents unfinished content from building up prematurely in search engines. After the domain move, editorial approval, legal review and technical quality assurance, indexing can be opened deliberately.

AI transparency will grow with the product. When new functions such as extended Companion conversations, relationship modules, profile refinement, export or additional EU language versions go live, this page must be updated accordingly.
